title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 — Practice Test 3
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is 15 + 27 - 12?
A honeycomb has 6 hexagonal cells arranged in a ring around one center cell. How many cells are there in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 200 180" width="200" height="180" font-family="sans-serif"><polygon points="100,70 120,80 120,100 100,110 80,100 80,80" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="100,40 120,50 120,70 100,80 80,70 80,50" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="140,55 160,65 160,85 140,95 120,85 120,65" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="140,95 160,105 160,125 140,135 120,125 120,105" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="100,110 120,120 120,140 100,150 80,140 80,120" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="60,95 80,105 80,125 60,135 40,125 40,105" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="60,55 80,65 80,85 60,95 40,85 40,65" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/></svg>
questions.image_description: A honeycomb with 1 center cell surrounded by 6 cells.

questions.question_text: Tom has 3 bags with 8 marbles each. He gives 5 marbles away. How many are left?

questions.question_text: Which of these numbers is both even and greater than 20?
How many small squares are in this 3x3 grid?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 180 180" width="180" height="180" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="20" y="20" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="60" y="20" width="40" height="40" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="100" y="20" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="20" y="60" width="40" height="40" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="60" y="60" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="100" y="60" width="40" height="40" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="20" y="100" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="60" y="100" width="40" height="40" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="100" y="100" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/></svg>

questions.question_text: A book has 60 pages. Lily reads 15 pages each day. How many days to finish?
Look at the pattern: 2, 4, 8, 16, __. What is the next number?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 260 60" width="260" height="60" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="10" y="15" width="40" height="30" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="30" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14">2</text><rect x="60" y="15" width="40" height="30" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="80" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14">4</text><rect x="110" y="15" width="40" height="30" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="130" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14">8</text><rect x="160" y="15" width="40" height="30" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="180" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14">16</text><rect x="210" y="15" width="40" height="30" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="230" y="35" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14">?</text></svg>

questions.question_text: Anna has 4 red, 3 blue, and 5 green pencils. How many pencils total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 260 80" width="260" height="80" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="10" y="30" width="70" height="20" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="45" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">Red: 4</text><rect x="90" y="30" width="70" height="20" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="125" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">Blue: 3</text><rect x="170" y="30" width="80" height="20" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="210" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">Green: 5</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Groups of pencils labeled Red 4, Blue 3, Green 5.

questions.question_text: In a room, Alex always tells the truth and Ben always lies. Ben says: 'Alex is taller than me.' Who is actually taller?
questions.solution: Ben lies, so the opposite is true: Ben is taller than Alex.

questions.question_text: How many triangles can you count in the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 200 180" width="200" height="180" font-family="sans-serif"><polygon points="100,20 30,150 170,150" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="100" y1="20" x2="100" y2="150" stroke="currentColor"/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided by a vertical line from apex to base.
questions.solution: Two small triangles plus the whole triangle = 3 triangles.

questions.question_text: A farmer has chickens and cows totalling 10 animals with 28 legs total. How many cows?
questions.solution: If all 10 were chickens: 20 legs. Extra legs: 28-20=8. Each cow adds 2 extra legs, so 8/2 = 4 cows.

questions.question_text: A 3x3 grid has numbers 1-9 placed so each row sums to 15. If the middle row has 4, 5, 6, what do 4+5+6 equal?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 180 180" width="180" height="180" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="20" y="20" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="60" y="20" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="100" y="20" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="20" y="60" width="40" height="40" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="60" y="60" width="40" height="40" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="100" y="60" width="40" height="40" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="20" y="100" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="60" y="100" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="100" y="100" width="40" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="40" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16">4</text><text x="80" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16">5</text><text x="120" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="16">6</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A 3x3 grid with middle row showing 4, 5, 6.

questions.question_text: What is 7 x 8 - 6?
A piece of rope is 24 m long. It is cut into 3 equal pieces. Each piece is cut in half. How long is each final piece?
Three kids sit in a row. Maya is not on the left. Tim is not next to Maya. If Sam is in the middle, where is Maya?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 240 80" width="240" height="80" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="20" y="20" width="50" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="45" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">?</text><rect x="90" y="20" width="50" height="40" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="115" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">Sam</text><rect x="160" y="20" width="50" height="40" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="185" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three seats with Sam in the middle.
questions.solution: Sam is in the middle. Maya is not on left, so Maya is on the right; Tim is on the left (not next to Maya).

questions.question_text: Pattern: 1, 3, 6, 10, 15, __. What comes next?
Three friends say: Ann: 'I am not first.' Bea: 'I am not last.' Cara: 'I am not second.' Only one lies. They finished in positions 1, 2, 3. If Cara finished second, who lied?
questions.solution: If Cara is second, then Cara's statement 'I am not second' is false. Cara lied.

questions.question_text: A square is divided into 4 equal smaller squares, and one of those is divided into 4 equal even smaller squares. How many squares total appear in the figure (all sizes)?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 180 180" width="180" height="180" font-family="sans-serif"><rect x="20" y="20" width="140" height="140" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="90" y1="20" x2="90" y2="160" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="20" y1="90" x2="160" y2="90" stroke="currentColor"/><rect x="90" y="20" width="70" height="70" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="125" y1="20" x2="125" y2="90" stroke="currentColor"/><line x1="90" y1="55" x2="160" y2="55" stroke="currentColor"/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large square split into 4, with the top-right quarter split into 4 smaller squares.

questions.question_text: Mia is twice as old as her sister. In 5 years, their ages sum to 25. How old is Mia now?
questions.solution: Let sister = x. Mia = 2x. In 5 years: (x+5)+(2x+5)=25, so 3x+10=25, x=5. Mia = 10.

questions.question_text: A bee moves through a honeycomb from cell A to cell B, moving only to an adjacent cell. The path has 4 cells (including A and B). If A and B are 3 cells apart, is this path the shortest?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 260 120" width="260" height="120" font-family="sans-serif"><polygon points="30,40 50,50 50,70 30,80 10,70 10,50" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="70,40 90,50 90,70 70,80 50,70 50,50" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="110,40 130,50 130,70 110,80 90,70 90,50" fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="currentColor"/><polygon points="150,40 170,50 170,70 150,80 130,70 130,50" fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="currentColor"/><text x="30" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14">A</text><text x="150" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="14">B</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four hexagonal cells in a row, with A leftmost and B rightmost.
questions.solution: 3 moves between 4 cells is the minimum since A and B are 3 apart.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of all odd numbers from 1 to 11?
A candy box has 3 layers. Each layer has 2 more candies than the one above. The top has 5. Total candies?
In a village, knights always tell the truth and knaves always lie. You meet 3 people. The first says: 'We are all knaves.' What is the first person?
questions.solution: If first were a knight, his statement would be true, meaning he is a knave — contradiction. So he is a knave.

questions.question_text: A train leaves at 9:15 and travels for 2 hours 50 minutes. What time does it arrive?
